display_name: Icon-class fleet (Icon, Star, Legend; Icon 4-7 on order)

key_uncertainties: fall 2023 debut timing
feature set differentiation versus Oasis class
initial booking response
2022-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon of the Seas construction reached a pivotal milestone ahead of a fall 2023 debut, positioned with reimagined signature features as the lead ship of a new class.

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

key_uncertainties: sustained pricing power after launch week
share gain from land-based family destinations
ramp of new neighborhoods such as Surfside
2022-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Detailed reveal of Icon of the Seas with eight distinct neighborhoods and a late-2023 delivery for a January 2024 debut. First week of bookings described as phenomenal, with Icon framed as the first ship of a new family-focused class for Royal Caribbean alongside Perfect Day.

key_uncertainties: durability of record booking pace into 2024
extension of premium pricing across cabin categories
comparable for Icon-class capacity additions
2022-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon of the Seas described as the best-selling product in company history since opening for sale, with the Ultimate Family Townhouse 55% sold for 2024 at an average $75,000 per week. Icon called out as the first new Royal Caribbean class in nine years and a major driver of 2024 demand.

key_uncertainties: yield contribution sizing within 2024
absolute load factor and rate premium versus prior classes
Hideaway Beach readiness for Icon's Caribbean season
2023-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon of the Seas significantly more booked for her inaugural season at materially higher rates than any prior Royal Caribbean ship launch despite only five months on sale. Caribbean debut in January 2024 with itineraries paired to Perfect Day at CocoCay and the new Hideaway Beach.

key_uncertainties: yield premium versus existing top products
ramp profile of inaugural Caribbean season
demand sustainability across 2024-2025
2023-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon of the Seas delivery scheduled for late Q4 with revenue sailings beginning at the end of January 2024. Management positions Icon and Utopia as effectively closing the gap to land-based resort experiences and notes Icon is breaking prior launch records.

key_uncertainties: 2024 yield contribution from Icon plus Utopia and Silver Ray
cost per APCD impact of new hardware
Hideaway Beach throughput at scale
2023-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Delivery later in Q3 with revenue sailings starting end of January 2024. 2024 capacity guided up 8% on the introduction of Icon, Utopia, and Silver Ray, with Icon described as the best-selling product in company history.

key_uncertainties: sustainability of premium pricing beyond inaugural season
applicability of Icon features to existing classes
implications for future newbuild cadence
2023-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon of the Seas welcomed first revenue guests in late January 2024 after a seven-year development. Management contextualizes Icon, Celebrity Ascent, and Silver Nova as drivers of the 8.5% 2024 capacity growth alongside Utopia and Silver Ray.

key_uncertainties: full-year financial contribution sizing
guest satisfaction durability after wave season
read-across to Star of the Seas booking curve
2024-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon described as exceeding lofty expectations on both guest satisfaction and financial performance since launch, with wave season bookings consistently at higher prices than 2023. Management characterizes the success as beyond expectations.

key_uncertainties: repeatability of Icon-level yields on Star and the third Icon-class ship
sustained load factor above design
capital allocation implications of accelerating Icon orders
2024-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon currently sailing at roughly 132% load factor. Order book extended with Star of the Seas in mid-2025 and a third Icon-class ship in 2026, alongside Celebrity Xcel and an Oasis 7 in 2028.

key_uncertainties: incremental return profile of the fourth Icon-class ship
shore-power and energy-transition capex per ship
port infrastructure readiness for Oasis and Icon-class scale
2024-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Announced a fourth Icon-class ship for 2027 delivery on the back of Icon's market response and anticipation for Star. Icon begins shore power at Port of Miami; class continues to exceed expectations on satisfaction and financial performance.

key_uncertainties: comp difficulty in 2025 against Icon launch year
first-quarter timing benefit sizing
size and economics of the future Discovery class versus Icon
2024-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Icon and Utopia performing well above expectations into 2025 with Q1 yield disproportionately benefiting from a full quarter of Icon. Management flags 2027 as the year Icon 4 joins and clarifies that the future Discovery class will be smaller than Icon.

key_uncertainties: pace of load-factor ramp on Star versus Icon's 2024 curve
yield uplift attributable to adding one Icon-class ship per year
comp dynamics as Icon laps its inaugural year
2025-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Star of the Seas, the second Icon-class ship, plus Celebrity Xcel exceeding expectations on pricing and load factors. 2025 capacity guided up 5.5% on Star, Xcel, and a full year of Icon, Utopia, and Silver Ray.

key_uncertainties: sizing of Icon 4 full-year benefit within 2028 Perfecta
deployment of Legend in Europe
integration with Perfect Day Mexico and Royal Beach Club roll-outs
2025-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Pipeline of seven new ships over the next several years framed around Star (2025), Legend of the Seas (2026), and Icon 4 (2027), with Icon 4 a named 2028 full-year contributor in Perfecta.

key_uncertainties: timing and economics of Icon 5 and the optional seventh Icon-class ship
transition risk into the post-Icon Discovery class
shipyard slot allocation through 2030
2025-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Confirmed Icon 5 order for 2028 delivery, added an option for a seventh Icon-class ship, and positioned the company for a new game-changing class beyond Icon.

key_uncertainties: European pricing for Legend versus Caribbean Icon and Star comps
organic-versus-inorganic yield contribution from each new Icon ship
competitive response to a three-Icon-class fleet
2025-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Legend of the Seas, the first Icon-class ship to debut in Europe, in a strong book position with very strong booking trends. Hardware platform now characterized as six Oasis-class plus three Icon-class ships, with Icon framed as having changed the game in 2024.

key_uncertainties: Legend pricing premium relative to Icon and Star
incremental margin from the Icon 6 and 7 orders
deployment mix between Perfect Day Mexico, Royal Beach Club Cozumel, and Icon-class ships
2026-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Announced orders for Icon 6 and Icon 7 citing confidence in the Icon platform's guest experience and economics. Legend of the Seas, the third Icon-class ship, in a strong book position with prices above those seen for Icon and Star and positioned alongside Perfect Day Mexico and Royal Beach Club Cozumel in the Western Caribbean strategy.

asked_by_history.framing: Anecdotal share-shift from land-based family destinations (Disney) to Icon as evidence of category capture
Acknowledged Icon as a recurring topic, signaling repeat franchise-level interest in the launch
Ranking of Icon, CocoCay expansion, and China reopening as 2024 yield tailwinds and any dilutive offsets
Pairing of Utopia and Icon deployments at CocoCay and implications for repeat visitation
Effect of Icon on 2024 NCC-ex-fuel through APCD growth and how China offsets play in
Decomposition of 2024 yield guidance including Icon hardware, occupancy ramp, and onboard
Balance-of-year European yield cadence given Hideaway Beach and Icon sailing patterns
How Icon's launch reception compares to prior new-class launches (Oasis, Quantum) and durability through Utopia and Star
Whether Icon's reception changes newbuild ordering pace, delivery cadence, or fleet rationalization decisions
Whether successful Icon features will be retrofitted onto Vision, Radiance, Quantum, or Oasis-class ships
Hardware allocation strategy between Icon-style week-long family vacations and Utopia-style short getaway product
Whether 2028 earnings growth at least matches Perfecta given Oasis-class, Icon, river, and private-destination launches
Organic versus inorganic decomposition of 2026 growth from another Icon-class ship plus calendar benefits from Icon and Celebrity launches
Trade-off between the smaller Discovery class and the larger Icon and Oasis ships in terms of yield versus capacity growth

primary_framings_observed: Yield uplift sizing from each incremental Icon-class delivery
Pricing premium and load-factor durability versus prior new-class launches
Family and multigenerational market share capture from land-based competitors
Cost-per-APCD impact of new hardware on NCC-ex-fuel cadence
Capital allocation: implications for newbuild ordering pace and fleet rationalization
Pairing of Icon-class deployment with Perfect Day, Hideaway Beach, and future Western Caribbean private destinations
Comparability of Icon-class economics to the planned smaller Discovery class

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: Quantified pricing premium and load-factor profile for Legend in Europe versus Icon and Star in the Caribbean
Marginal yield and earnings contribution per incremental Icon-class ship (Icon 4 through Icon 7) within Perfecta and beyond
Capex per ship and energy-transition spend (shore power, alternative fuels) on later Icon-class deliveries
Strategic rationale and economics for Icon 6 and Icon 7 relative to the planned smaller Discovery class
Whether Icon-class load factors structurally above 130 percent persist as the class scales to five or more ships
How Icon-class deployment is sequenced with Perfect Day Mexico and Royal Beach Club Cozumel in the Western Caribbean
Read-across of Icon features to existing Oasis, Quantum, Vision, and Radiance ships through revitalization capex
